competencies.competency_text: Describes relationships (including between humans and animals/nature) and traditions (art forms, celebrations, festivals) in the family and community
competencies.justification: Students describe connections between community members and places (shopkeeper, health centre, school) in 'A Tour Around My Village', describe festival traditions of Van Mahotsav, the Uruka/Magh Bihu feast with Bhela Ghar in 'Sharing a Meal', and explore the Khetala agricultural cooperation tradition of Sikkim in 'Let us reflect'.
sections: Van Mahotsav: A Festival for Planting Trees

competencies.competency_text: Explains the functioning of local institutions (family, school, bank/post office, market, and panchayat) in different forms (story, drawing, tabulating data, reports), and analyses their roles
competencies.justification: In 'A Tour Around My Village', students complete a structured table recording how local institutions (health centre, police, school, shopkeeper, road) function and their community roles. In 'Let us reflect', students map the roles of different people in their school institution through a spider-diagram write activity.

competencies.competency_text: Describes the relationship between the natural environment and cultural practices in their immediate environment (nature of work, food, festivals, traditions)
competencies.justification: Van Mahotsav is presented as a cultural festival whose practice (tree planting, park restoration) is directly tied to the natural environment and the rainy season. 'Sharing a Meal' describes the banana-leaf feast — a cultural food practice using a natural resource — and the Uruka/Magh Bihu harvest festival in Assam. 'Let us reflect' presents the Khetala practice in Sikkim — agricultural specialists who cooperate at harvest time, connecting seasonal natural cycles to community cultural work.
sections: Van Mahotsav: A Festival for Planting Trees

competencies.competency_text: Demonstrates how natural resources can be shared, maintained, and conserved (trees, use of rainwater, benefits of millets)
competencies.justification: The entire Van Mahotsav narrative is built around tree planting and maintaining a shared public park as a natural resource. In 'Helping Hands in the Community', community members repair the park and plant saplings along its boundary. In 'Celebrating Van Mahotsav', the park is collectively restored and maintained through cooperative effort. In 'Sharing a Meal', banana leaves are composted in a pit to make manure, demonstrating natural resource conservation through traditional practice.
sections: Van Mahotsav: A Festival for Planting Trees

competencies.competency_text: Learns about basic social and behavioural norms, values, and dispositions that benefit our social and natural environments and that help our society function smoothly (using dustbins, standing in queues, conserving water, using public transportation, keeping one's environment clean, always helping others in need regardless of background)
competencies.justification: In 'Helping Hands in the Community', every villager contributes their specific skill regardless of background (mason, electrician, nursery owner, carpenter) — the chapter architecturally compels students to recognise and value this cooperative norm. 'Celebrating Van Mahotsav' shows organised community groups cleaning, planting, and cooking together — enacting the norm of keeping the environment clean and helping others. In 'Let us reflect', group activity tasks (cleanliness drive, tree plantation) directly involve students in performing these civic dispositions.

competencies.competency_text: Performs simple inquiry related to specific questions independently or in groups
competencies.justification: In 'Helping Hands in the Community', students are given structured guiding questions and asked to conduct an inquiry by interviewing family members or neighbours about their work (T-9). In 'Let us reflect', students perform an inquiry about local traditional skills and crafts by asking family members (T-20), and conduct a group-based situational inquiry to identify a community problem and plan an improvement activity (T-19).
